_comment: GraphSmith Phase A CI suite manifest (contracts/11, task A-ci-fix B3).
Every tests/<component>/<tester>/run-tests.js suite discovered by the CI
workflow (.github/workflows/ci.yml, mirrored in
ci-templates/gitlab-ci.yml) is looked up here by path-prefix to decide
whether it GATES the merge or is EVIDENCE-ONLY (run + logged, never
blocks).
RULE: a per-component REGRESSION suite under tests/<component>/<family>/
for a real GraphSmith component (gate, promote, verify, scenario,
manifest, state-store, loaders, lab, ...) is GATING -- it must stay
green, it is the regression proof for that component. The STANDING
attack batteries under tests/attacks/{constitutional,toctou,
module-escape}/ are also GATING -- they are permanent security
regression tests, not one-shot review artifacts.
Only the ONE-SHOT, NEGATIVE-CONTROL / ADVERSARIAL-REVIEW harnesses --
tests/attacks/{deepseek,gpt-sol-pro}/ and tests/ci/{deepseek,
gpt-sol-pro}/ -- are EVIDENCE-ONLY. Those harnesses exit non-zero BY
DESIGN when they report real findings (that is the whole point of an
adversarial review); gating merge on them would turn CI red on
evidence artifacts even though the component(s) under review are
otherwise TEST-PASSED. Their output is still executed and printed in
full every run -- findings are never hidden -- they just do not flip
the job's exit code.
FAIL-SAFE: a discovered tests/**/run-tests.js suite that appears in
NEITHER list below is treated as GATING and the runner prints an
explicit warning naming it. Unknown suites are never silently
downgraded to evidence-only -- only a suite explicitly listed here as
evidence_only is exempted from gating.
